title: TransEg — Local Digital Twin Reference Implementation (v0.1.0)
description: First executable reference implementation of the TransEg architecture: a fully local, privacy-preserving, modular, containerized identity-continuity research platform. Not a chatbot; not merely a talking avatar. Four typed layers (Human Identity, Representation, Digital Twin, TransEg — never conflated), an identity graph with per-node provenance/timestamps/confidence/permissions, a typed Staggered Upload state machine (stages 0-7, independently enabled/revoked/versioned/auditable), and a replaceable component pipeline (Silero VAD, Whisper.cpp ASR, Ollama capability-contract LLM, Kokoro TTS, FasterLivePortrait avatar as sole GPU tenant on a 4 GB VRAM budget) behind a FastAPI gateway. TransEg exists within Zistgah, the enduring institutional substrate; PEDLER provides adaptive cognition, PRATIK the execution substrate, PAT.AL societal participation, VIDYA the knowledge ecosystem. Derived from PEDLER (first published November 2001). Sole author: Abhishek Choudhary, AyeAI, Hyderabad, India. Software GPL-3.0-or-later; content CC-BY-SA-4.0; metadata CC0-1.0; novel methods proprietary (patents reserved).
